zh.lifeSummary: Sir Arnaud de Gaveston是Garisie de Gaveston的兒子，於1247年出生在法國米迪-列內斯。他於大約1274年與Lady Clarmunda De Louvigny在法國貝阿結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1302年5月18日在英國英格蘭哈普爾維古姆享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭哈普爾維古姆維古姆 凱德拉爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Arnaud de Gaveston是Garisie de Gaveston的儿子，于1247年出生在法国米迪-列內斯。他于大约1274年与Lady Clarmunda De Louvigny在法国貝阿结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1302年5月18日在英国英格兰哈普爾維古姆享年55岁，埋葬在英国英格兰哈普爾維古姆維古姆 凱德拉爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gaveston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: probably an altered form of   Graveson  , hypercorrecting the process seen in   Urmson   from Urmston, Lancs.

variants.name: Graveson
meanings: English: relationship name, ‘son of the steward, farm bailiff, or village headman’, largely confined to northern England. From the north Midlands through Yorks to the north-west, the source is Middle English greyve, grayve, greve, a borrowing of Old Scandinavian greifi, see Grave . In Durham and Northumb the source is Middle English greve, from Old (Northumbrian) English grǣfa, see Grieve . The two forms are difficult to tell apart, except that the post-medieval spelling Grieve- usually reflects the Northumbrian term. Pronunciation developments have sometimes obscured the origin of the name. Grayveson frequently lost the -v-, becoming Gra(y)son and Greeson; see Grayson .

variants.name: Grayson
meanings: English (northern): variant of Graveson, ‘son of the steward, farm bailiff, or village headman’. From the North Midlands through Yorkshire to the north-west, the source is Middle English grayve, grayve, greve, a borrowing of Old Norse greifi ‘earl, count’ (see Grave ). In Durham and Northumberland the source is Middle English greve, from Old (Northumbrian) English grǣfa with the same meaning (see Grieve ).
English: possibly a habitational name from Greysouthen (Cumbria), recorded as Graysone in 1505.

nameMeanings.name: Arnaud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Arnaud de Gaveston was born in 1247, in Midi-Pyrénées, France as the son of Garisie de Gaveston. He married Lady Clarmunda De Louvigny about 1274, in Béarn, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 18 May 1302, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 55, and was buried in Winchester Cathedral, Winchester, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Arnaud de Gaveston est né en 1247, à Midi-Pyrénées, France, fils de Garisie de Gaveston. Il a épousé Lady Clarmunda De Louvigny environ 1274, à Béarn, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 18 mai 1302, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Winchester Cathedral, Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 18. ko.lifeSummary: Sir Arnaud de Gaveston(은)는 1247년 프랑스 미디-p이ee스에서 Garisie de Gaveston의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1274년 프랑스 브e아른에서 Lady Clarmunda De Louvigny(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1302년 5월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 윈체스터에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 윈체스터 윈체스터 캐테드럴에 묻혔다.
